Question Text: Convert 144 km/h to m/s.
Feedback:
This conversion wasn’t included in lesson. (Km to miles) might’ve good to touch on as there are usually a few questions in the test that relate to various measurements and whether you know how to convert them. 

Also, really liked the sanity check in the lesson (walking speed around 5mph.

Another note, I think you would be more likely to see mph as an abbreviated unit than m/h but might be wrong.

Question Text: Convert 198 km/h to m/s.
Feedback:
Would be helpful to specify if m/s is miles or meters per second, I think writing out in full sentence with abbreviations in brackets could be helpful. 
Could also include in the explanation after answering how you might do that sum without a calculator.
Eg.
Where the numbers came from, 1000 meters in a kilometre.

198*1000/3600

You can cancel out the zeros, so becomes

198*10/36

Then 

198*10=1,980 

So 1980/36

Then maybe a lightbulb or hint reminding them to use long division 

Only being picky as some of this is assumed knowledge and not in the lesson.
